campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies in Campbell have to pay. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to these tax rates, and the council discussed and voted on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased or changed
Finance department presented the annual tax rate update
Council will vote on the new rates to take effect in the next fiscal year
Emergency preparedness office was also involved in the meeting

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could comment on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward with the ordinance by skipping the standard first reading step and planning to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance being fast-tracked for approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
Standard reading procedures waived to expedite process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in April and review the city's finances for February. The council also approved several construction contracts and agreements to move forward with various town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed city's February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other service providers
Approved town improvement projects and professional service agreements

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a procedural meeting to organize upcoming agendas for May 5 and May 12, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to each meeting's agenda, decided which topics need specific time slots, and checked on any required public notice waivers. The council also reviewed public records from mid-to-late April.
keyTopics: Finalized May 5 meeting agenda with any new items and time assignments
Organized May 12 draft agenda with scheduling priorities
Reviewed public records from April 16-23, 2026
Checked notice requirements for agenda items and documents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear from residents during public comment. The main focus was reviewing and deciding which neighborhood improvement projects should receive funding for the 2026-27 budget year.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comments from Mountain View residents
Approval of previous meeting minutes
Community attendance at neighborhood meetings
Staff and committee reports on city initiatives

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to provide public comments either in person at City Hall or online during the meeting.
keyTopics: Board and Commission candidate interviews
Public comment period (in-person and online options)
Study session format for council review

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 30, 2026 to approve meeting minutes from the Economic Development Committee's January and March sessions. The council also opened the floor for residents to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each, though they cannot make decisions on topics not officially on the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of January 29, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Approval of March 27, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Public comment period for residents on any committee jurisdiction matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be put on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup games happening in the city. The council also approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters that will run through 2029. One item that was originally on the agenda was removed before the meeting started.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs permitted around Levi's Stadium for FIFA World Cup 2026
New 3-year contract approved with Santa Clara firefighters union
One agenda item withdrawn before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to approve the city's next fiscal year budget and fees, discuss homelessness initiatives with Santa Clara County, and handle routine administrative matters. Residents had opportunities to speak during public hearings about tax policies, development fees, and other city user charges.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget and development fees approval
Tax equity and fiscal responsibility policy reissuance
County homelessness prevention and housing plan
Updates to city user fees and service charges
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several routine matters including administrative updates and leadership elections. The main focus was a public hearing on a proposal to divide a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into nine residential lots, which would allow construction of nine new single-family homes and seven smaller accessory dwelling units on the property.
keyTopics: Subdivision proposal for 4103 Old Trace Road - creating 9 new single-family homes
Addition of 7 junior accessory dwelling units (smaller rental units) on the same property
Election of new City Council Chair and Vice Chair
Project is exempt from environmental review under state law
Property zoned as Residential Estate district
